<file path="ui/src/lib/components/panels/LayerPanel.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
  import { useLayerStore } from '$lib/stores/layerStore';
  import type { LayerEntry, LayerState } from '$lib/stores/layerStore';
  import { Eye, EyeOff, Trash2 } from 'lucide-svelte';
  
  // Get the store
  const layerStore = useLayerStore;
  
  // Subscribe to layers and selection
  let layers = $state<LayerEntry[]>([]);
  let selectedLayerId = $state<string | null>(null);
  
  // Subscribe to store changes
  $effect(() => {
    const unsubscribe = layerStore.subscribe((state: LayerState) => {
      layers = state.layers;
      selectedLayerId = state.selectedLayerId;
    });
    
    return unsubscribe;
  });
  
  // Helper to get layer ID
  function getLayerId(entry: LayerEntry): string {
    if ('Volume' in entry.spec) {
      return entry.spec.Volume.id;
    }
    // Add other layer types as needed
    return '';
  }
  
  // Helper to get layer name
  function getLayerName(entry: LayerEntry): string {
    if ('Volume' in entry.spec) {
      // Use source_resource_id as a temporary name
      // In a real app, this would come from volume metadata
      return `Volume ${entry.spec.Volume.source_resource_id.slice(0, 8)}`;
    }
    return 'Unknown Layer';
  }
  
  // Layer visibility tracking (local state for now)
  let layerVisibility = $state<Record<string, boolean>>({});
  
  function toggleVisibility(layerId: string) {
    layerVisibility[layerId] = !(layerVisibility[layerId] ?? true);
    // TODO: Update render state when we have visibility control
  }
  
  function selectLayer(layerId: string) {
    layerStore.getState().selectLayer(layerId);
  }
  
  function removeLayer(layerId: string) {
    layerStore.getState().removeLayer(layerId);
  }
</script>

<div class="layer-panel">
  <div class="panel-header">
    <h3 class="text-sm font-semibold">Layers</h3>
    <span class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">{layers.length} active</span>
  </div>
  
  <div class="layer-list">
    {#if layers.length === 0}
      <div class="empty-state">
        <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">No layers loaded</p>
        <p class="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-1">
          Drop files in the tree browser to add layers
        </p>
      </div>
    {:else}
      {#each layers as layer}
        {@const layerId = getLayerId(layer)}
        {@const isVisible = layerVisibility[layerId] ?? true}
        {@const isSelected = selectedLayerId === layerId}
        
        <div 
          class="layer-item"
          class:selected={isSelected}
          class:loading={layer.isLoadingGpu}
          class:error={!!layer.error}
        >
          <button
            class="layer-content"
            onclick={() => selectLayer(layerId)}
          >
            <div class="layer-info">
              <span class="layer-name">{getLayerName(layer)}</span>
              {#if layer.isLoadingGpu}
                <span class="layer-status">Loading...</span>
              {:else if layer.error}
                <span class="layer-status error">Error</span>
              {:else if layer.gpu}
                <span class="layer-status ready">Ready</span>
              {/if}
            </div>
          </button>
          
          <div class="layer-actions">
            <button
              class="icon-button"
              onclick={() => toggleVisibility(layerId)}
              title={isVisible ? 'Hide layer' : 'Show layer'}
            >
              {#if isVisible}
                <Eye size={16} />
              {:else}
                <EyeOff size={16} />
              {/if}
            </button>
            
            <button
              class="icon-button danger"
              onclick={() => removeLayer(layerId)}
              title="Remove layer"
            >
              <Trash2 size={16} />
            </button>
          </div>
        </div>
      {/each}
    {/if}
  </div>
</div>

  }

  }

    background: var(--destructive);
    color: var(--destructive-foreground);
  }
  
  /* Custom scrollbar */
  .layer-list::-webkit-scrollbar {
    width: 0.5rem;
  }
  
  .layer-list::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
    background: var(--muted);
  }
  
  .layer-list::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
    background: var(--border);
    border-radius: 0.25rem;
  }
  
  .layer-list::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
    background: var(--muted-foreground);

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/panels/PlaceholderPanel.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
  // Simple placeholder panel component
  export let title: string = 'Placeholder';
  export let componentType: string = 'unknown';
</script>

<div class="placeholder-panel">
  <h2>{title}</h2>
  <p>Content for: {componentType}</p>
  <p>(This is a placeholder Svelte component)</p>
</div>

<style>
  .placeholder-panel {
    padding: 10px;
    height: 100%;
    overflow: auto;
    background-color: #eee;
  }
  h2 {
    margin-top: 0;
    font-size: 1.1em;

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/ui/DualRangeSlider.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
  import { createEventDispatcher } from 'svelte';
  
  interface Props {
    min?: number;
    max?: number;
    step?: number;
    valueLow?: number;
    valueHigh?: number;
    disabled?: boolean;
    label?: string;
    formatValue?: (value: number) => string;
  }
  
  let {
    min = 0,
    max = 1,
    step = 0.01,
    valueLow = $bindable(0.2),
    valueHigh = $bindable(0.8),
    disabled = false,
    label = '',
    formatValue = (v: number) => v.toFixed(2)
  }: Props = $props();
  
  const dispatch = createEventDispatcher<{
    change: { low: number; high: number };
    input: { low: number; high: number };
  }>();
  
  // Track which thumb is being dragged
  let draggingThumb: 'low' | 'high' | null = $state(null);
  
  // Calculate thumb positions as percentages
  let lowPercent = $derived(((valueLow - min) / (max - min)) * 100);
  let highPercent = $derived(((valueHigh - min) / (max - min)) * 100);
  
  // Handle low thumb input
  function handleLowInput(e: Event) {
    const target = e.target as HTMLInputElement;
    const newValue = parseFloat(target.value);
    
    // Ensure low doesn't exceed high
    valueLow = Math.min(newValue, valueHigh);
    
    dispatch('input', { low: valueLow, high: valueHigh });
  }
  
  // Handle high thumb input
  function handleHighInput(e: Event) {
    const target = e.target as HTMLInputElement;
    const newValue = parseFloat(target.value);
    
    // Ensure high doesn't go below low
    valueHigh = Math.max(newValue, valueLow);
    
    dispatch('input', { low: valueLow, high: valueHigh });
  }
  
  // Handle change events (when user releases the thumb)
  function handleChange() {
    dispatch('change', { low: valueLow, high: valueHigh });
  }
  
  // Track which thumb is being dragged for z-index management
  function startDrag(thumb: 'low' | 'high') {
    draggingThumb = thumb;
  }
  
  function endDrag() {
    draggingThumb = null;
  }
</script>

<div class="dual-range-slider" class:disabled>
  {#if label}
    <label class="slider-label">
      <span>{label}</span>
      <span class="values">
        {formatValue(valueLow)} - {formatValue(valueHigh)}
      </span>
    </label>
  {/if}
  
  <div class="slider-container">
    <!-- Track background -->
    <div class="track"></div>
    
    <!-- Active range track -->
    <div 
      class="track-active"
      style="left: {lowPercent}%; right: {100 - highPercent}%"
    ></div>
    
    <!-- Low value input -->
    <input
      type="range"
      class="thumb thumb-low"
      class:dragging={draggingThumb === 'low'}
      {min}
      {max}
      {step}
      value={valueLow}
      {disabled}
      oninput={handleLowInput}
      onchange={handleChange}
      onmousedown={() => startDrag('low')}
      onmouseup={endDrag}
      ontouchstart={() => startDrag('low')}
      ontouchend={endDrag}
    />
    
    <!-- High value input -->
    <input
      type="range"
      class="thumb thumb-high"
      class:dragging={draggingThumb === 'high'}
      {min}
      {max}
      {step}
      value={valueHigh}
      {disabled}
      oninput={handleHighInput}
      onchange={handleChange}
      onmousedown={() => startDrag('high')}
      onmouseup={endDrag}
      ontouchstart={() => startDrag('high')}
      ontouchend={endDrag}
    />
  </div>
</div>

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/ui/ThresholdInfo.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
  import { BarChart3 } from 'lucide-svelte';
  
  interface Props {
    thresholdLow: number;
    thresholdHigh: number;
    dataMin: number;
    dataMax: number;
    showHistogram?: boolean;
  }
  
  let {
    thresholdLow,
    thresholdHigh,
    dataMin,
    dataMax,
    showHistogram = false
  }: Props = $props();
  
  // Calculate what percentage of the range is visible
  let visiblePercent = $derived(() => {
    const totalRange = dataMax - dataMin;
    const visibleRange = thresholdHigh - thresholdLow;
    return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (visibleRange / totalRange) * 100));
  });
  
  // Convert normalized threshold back to data values
  let dataThreshLow = $derived(dataMin + thresholdLow * (dataMax - dataMin));
  let dataThreshHigh = $derived(dataMin + thresholdHigh * (dataMax - dataMin));
</script>

<div class="threshold-info">
  <div class="info-header">
    <BarChart3 size={14} />
    <span class="info-title">Threshold Range</span>
  </div>
  
  <div class="info-content">
    <div class="range-display">
      <span class="range-value">{dataThreshLow.toFixed(2)}</span>
      <span class="range-separator">to</span>
      <span class="range-value">{dataThreshHigh.toFixed(2)}</span>
    </div>
    
    <div class="visibility-bar">
      <div 
        class="visibility-fill"
        style="width: {visiblePercent()}%"
      ></div>
    </div>
    
    <p class="visibility-text">
      Showing {visiblePercent().toFixed(0)}% of value range
    </p>
  </div>
  
  {#if showHistogram}
    <div class="histogram-placeholder">
      <p class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
        Histogram visualization coming soon
      </p>
    </div>
  {/if}
</div>

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/ui/ThresholdPresets.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
  import { createEventDispatcher } from 'svelte';
  import { Calculator } from 'lucide-svelte';
  
  interface Props {
    onSelect: (low: number, high: number) => void;
    dataMin?: number;
    dataMax?: number;
  }
  
  let {
    onSelect,
    dataMin = -5,
    dataMax = 5
  }: Props = $props();
  
  const dispatch = createEventDispatcher<{
    select: { low: number; high: number };
  }>();
  
  // Common statistical thresholds
  const presets = [
    { label: 't > 2.0', value: 2.0, description: 'p < 0.05 (one-tailed)' },
    { label: 't > 2.58', value: 2.58, description: 'p < 0.01 (one-tailed)' },
    { label: 't > 3.29', value: 3.29, description: 'p < 0.001 (one-tailed)' },
    { label: 'z > 1.96', value: 1.96, description: 'p < 0.05 (normal dist)' },
    { label: 'Custom Range', value: null, description: 'Set custom threshold range' },
  ];
  
  let showAbsolute = $state(true);
  let selectedPreset = $state<number | null>(null);
  
  function applyPreset(threshold: number | null) {
    selectedPreset = threshold;
    
    if (threshold === null) {
      // Custom range - don't change current values
      return;
    }
    
    if (showAbsolute) {
      // For absolute values, we want to hide values between -threshold and +threshold
      // This shows both strong positive and negative activations
      const normalizedNegThresh = normalizeValue(-threshold);
      const normalizedPosThresh = normalizeValue(threshold);
      
      // Current shader only supports showing values within a range
      // So we can either show:
      // 1. Positive values above threshold (most common for activation maps)
      // 2. Negative values below -threshold
      // Let's default to positive for now
      onSelect(normalizedPosThresh, 1);
      
      // TODO: Add a toggle for positive/negative/both
    } else {
      // Show values greater than threshold (one-tailed)
      const normalizedThreshold = normalizeValue(threshold);
      onSelect(normalizedThreshold, 1);
    }
    
    dispatch('select', { 
      low: showAbsolute ? normalizeValue(threshold) : normalizeValue(threshold), 
      high: 1 
    });
  }
  
  function normalizeValue(value: number): number {
    // Normalize value from data range to [0, 1]
    return (value - dataMin) / (dataMax - dataMin);
  }
  
  function clearPreset() {
    selectedPreset = null;
    onSelect(0, 1); // Show all values
    dispatch('select', { low: 0, high: 1 });
  }
</script>

<div class="threshold-presets">
  <div class="preset-header">
    <Calculator size={16} />
    <span class="preset-title">Statistical Thresholds</span>
  </div>
  
  <div class="preset-options">
    <label class="checkbox-label">
      <input
        type="checkbox"
        bind:checked={showAbsolute}
        class="checkbox"
      />
      <span>Show absolute values</span>
    </label>
  </div>
  
  <div class="preset-buttons">
    {#each presets as preset}
      <button
        class="preset-btn"
        class:active={selectedPreset === preset.value}
        onclick={() => applyPreset(preset.value)}
        title={preset.description}
      >
        {preset.label}
      </button>
    {/each}
  </div>
  
  {#if selectedPreset !== null}
    <button
      class="clear-btn"
      onclick={clearPreset}
    >
      Clear threshold
    </button>
  {/if}
  
  <div class="preset-note">
    <p class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
      {#if showAbsolute}
        Showing positive values above threshold. For negative activations, 
        uncheck "Show absolute values" and use negative threshold.
      {:else}
        Showing all values above the selected threshold.
      {/if}
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

  }
  
  .preset-header {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;

    margin-bottom: 0.75rem;

  }
  
  .preset-title {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    font-weight: 500;
  }

    margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
  }

    color: var(--muted-foreground);
    margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
  }
  
  .clear-btn:hover {
    background: var(--muted);
    color: var(--foreground);
  }

    margin-top: 0.75rem;
    padding-top: 0.75rem;
    border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
  }
</style>

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/views/OrthogonalView.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
	/**
	 * @component OrthogonalView
	 * @description Single orthogonal view canvas for displaying a specific slice orientation
	 */
	import { onMount, onDestroy } from 'svelte';
	import { debounce } from 'lodash-es';
	import { coreApi } from '$lib/api';
	import { useLayerStore, type LayerEntry } from '$lib/stores/layerStore';
	import { crosshairSlice } from '$lib/stores/crosshairSlice';
	import { zustandToReadable } from '$lib/stores/zustandBridge';
	import { ViewType, getViewTypeName } from '$lib/types/ViewType';
	import type { VolumeLayerGpuInfo, LayerSpec } from '$lib/api';

	// Props
	let { 
		layerId,
		viewType,
		onViewportChange
	}: { 
		layerId: string | null;
		viewType: ViewType;
		onViewportChange?: (viewType: ViewType, viewport: { scale: number; offset: [number, number] }) => void;
	} = $props();

	// State
	let canvasElement = $state<HTMLCanvasElement>();
	let viewScale = $state(1.0);
	let viewOffset = $state([0, 0] as [number, number]);
	let isDragging = $state(false);
	let lastPointerPos = $state<[number, number] | null>(null);
	let containerSize = $state({ width: 0, height: 0 });

	// Helper to get layer ID from spec
	function getLayerIdFromSpec(spec: LayerSpec): string | null {
		if (spec && 'Volume' in spec) {
			return spec.Volume.id;
		}
		return null;
	}
	
	// Get layer from store
	let layerEntry = $derived(
		layerId ? useLayerStore.getState().layers.find((l: LayerEntry) => getLayerIdFromSpec(l.spec) === layerId) : null
	);

	// Create reactive crosshair store
	const crosshairStore = zustandToReadable(crosshairSlice);

	// Canvas resize observer
	let resizeObserver: ResizeObserver | null = null;

	// Debounced resize handler
	const handleResizeDebounced = debounce((width: number, height: number) => {
		if (width > 0 && height > 0) {
			containerSize = { width, height };
			// TODO: Notify backend about canvas resize for this specific view
		}
	}, 250);

	// Mouse/pointer handlers
	function handlePointerDown(event: PointerEvent) {
		if (event.button === 0) { // Left click
			isDragging = true;
			lastPointerPos = [event.clientX, event.clientY];
			(event.target as HTMLElement).setPointerCapture(event.pointerId);
		}
	}

	function handlePointerMove(event: PointerEvent) {
		if (isDragging && lastPointerPos) {
			const dx = event.clientX - lastPointerPos[0];
			const dy = event.clientY - lastPointerPos[1];
			
			viewOffset = [
				viewOffset[0] + dx / viewScale,
				viewOffset[1] + dy / viewScale
			];
			
			lastPointerPos = [event.clientX, event.clientY];
			
			onViewportChange?.(viewType, { scale: viewScale, offset: viewOffset });
		}
	}

	function handlePointerUp(event: PointerEvent) {
		isDragging = false;
		lastPointerPos = null;
		(event.target as HTMLElement).releasePointerCapture(event.pointerId);
	}

	function handleWheel(event: WheelEvent) {
		const scaleFactor = event.deltaY > 0 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
		viewScale = Math.max(0.1, Math.min(10, viewScale * scaleFactor));
		
		onViewportChange?.(viewType, { scale: viewScale, offset: viewOffset });
	}

	// Rendering state
	let renderContext: CanvasRenderingContext2D | null = null;
	let animationFrameId: number | null = null;

	// Set up rendering when GPU resources are available
	$effect(() => {
		if (layerEntry?.gpu && canvasElement && !renderContext) {
			renderContext = canvasElement.getContext('2d');
			if (renderContext) {
				startRenderLoop();
			}
		}
	});

	// Update view when crosshair changes
	$effect(() => {
		// Subscribe to crosshair changes
		const crosshairCoords = $crosshairStore.crosshairWorldCoord;
		
		// Only update if we have GPU resources and a render context
		if (layerEntry?.gpu && renderContext && crosshairCoords) {
			// Update the view parameters with new crosshair position
			setupViewRenderParams();
		}
	});

	// Update view when viewType changes
	$effect(() => {
		// React to viewType changes
		viewType; // Access to track dependency
		
		// Only update if we have GPU resources and a render context
		if (layerEntry?.gpu && renderContext) {
			// Update the view parameters for new view type
			setupViewRenderParams();
		}
	});

	// Render loop
	async function startRenderLoop() {
		if (!layerEntry?.gpu || !renderContext) return;

		try {
			// Set up rendering parameters for this view
			await setupViewRenderParams();
			
			// Start continuous rendering
			const render = async () => {
				if (!layerEntry?.gpu) {
					stopRenderLoop();
					return;
				}

				try {
					await renderFrame();
					animationFrameId = requestAnimationFrame(render);
				} catch (err) {
					console.error(`Failed to render ${getViewTypeName(viewType)} view:`, err);
				}
			};

			render();
		} catch (err) {
			console.error(`Failed to start render loop for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}:`, err);
		}
	}

	function stopRenderLoop() {
		if (animationFrameId !== null) {
			cancelAnimationFrame(animationFrameId);
			animationFrameId = null;
		}
	}

	async function setupViewRenderParams() {
		if (!layerEntry?.gpu) return;

		try {
			// Get canvas dimensions
			const width = canvasElement?.width || 512;
			const height = canvasElement?.height || 512;

			// Ensure offscreen render target matches canvas size
			await coreApi.create_offscreen_render_target(width, height);

			// Get current crosshair position from store
			const crosshairCoords = $crosshairStore.crosshairWorldCoord || [0, 0, 0];

			// Map view type to plane ID
			let planeId: 0 | 1 | 2;
			switch (viewType) {
				case ViewType.Axial:
					planeId = 2;
					break;
				case ViewType.Coronal:
					planeId = 1;
					break;
				case ViewType.Sagittal:
					planeId = 0;
					break;
			}

			// Get volume dimensions from the spec to calculate view size in mm
			// For now, use the canvas dimensions as a reasonable default
			// TODO: Calculate actual physical view size based on volume spacing
			const viewWidthMm = width;
			const viewHeightMm = height;

			// Use the simplified API that handles all the complex frame parameter calculations
			await coreApi.update_frame_for_synchronized_view(
				viewWidthMm,
				viewHeightMm,
				crosshairCoords as [number, number, number],
				planeId
			);
			
			// Also need to render a frame
			await coreApi.render_frame();

			// Add the layer to render state if not already added
			const textureCoords = [
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.u_min,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.v_min,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.u_max,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.v_max
			];

			await coreApi.add_render_layer(
				layerEntry.gpu.atlas_layer_index,
				1.0, // Full opacity
				textureCoords
			);

		} catch (err) {
			console.error(`Failed to setup render params for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}:`, err);
		}
	}

	async function renderFrame() {
		if (!renderContext || !canvasElement) return;

		try {
			// Render to offscreen buffer and get image
			const imageDataUrl = await coreApi.render_to_image();
			
			// Parse the data URL
			if (imageDataUrl.startsWith('data:image/raw-rgba;base64,')) {
				// Convert raw RGBA to canvas
				const base64Data = imageDataUrl.substring('data:image/raw-rgba;base64,'.length);
				const binaryData = atob(base64Data);
				const bytes = new Uint8Array(binaryData.length);
				for (let i = 0; i < binaryData.length; i++) {
					bytes[i] = binaryData.charCodeAt(i);
				}

				// Create image data with proper dimensions
				const width = canvasElement.width;
				const height = canvasElement.height;
				
				console.log(`OrthogonalView: Received image data - size: ${bytes.length}, expected: ${width * height * 4}`);
				
				// Ensure we have the right amount of data
				if (bytes.length === width * height * 4) {
					// Check if all pixels are black
					let allBlack = true;
					for (let i = 0; i < bytes.length; i += 4) {
						if (bytes[i] !== 0 || bytes[i+1] !== 0 || bytes[i+2] !== 0 || bytes[i+3] !== 0) {
							allBlack = false;
							break;
						}
					}
					
					if (allBlack) {
						console.warn(`OrthogonalView: All pixels are black for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}`);
					}
					
					const imageData = new ImageData(new Uint8ClampedArray(bytes.buffer), width, height);
					renderContext.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
				} else {
					console.error(`OrthogonalView: Image data size mismatch - got ${bytes.length}, expected ${width * height * 4}`);
				}
			} else {
				console.error(`OrthogonalView: Unexpected image data format: ${imageDataUrl.substring(0, 50)}...`);
			}
		} catch (err) {
			console.error(`Failed to render frame for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}:`, err);
		}
	}

	// Update canvas size when container resizes
	function updateCanvasSize(width: number, height: number) {
		if (canvasElement) {
			canvasElement.width = width;
			canvasElement.height = height;
			// Re-setup render params with new dimensions
			if (layerEntry?.gpu) {
				setupViewRenderParams();
			}
		}
	}

	// Lifecycle
	onMount(() => {
		if (canvasElement) {
			resizeObserver = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
				const entry = entries[0];
				const { width, height } = entry.contentRect;
				updateCanvasSize(width, height);
				handleResizeDebounced(width, height);
			});
			resizeObserver.observe(canvasElement);

			// Set initial canvas size
			const rect = canvasElement.getBoundingClientRect();
			updateCanvasSize(rect.width, rect.height);
		}
	});

	onDestroy(() => {
		stopRenderLoop();
		resizeObserver?.disconnect();
		handleResizeDebounced.cancel();
	});
</script>

<div class="orthogonal-view" data-view-type={viewType}>
	<!-- View label -->
	<div class="view-label">{getViewTypeName(viewType)}</div>
	
	<!-- Error/loading overlays -->
	{#if !layerEntry}
		<div class="overlay loading">No layer selected</div>
	{:else if layerEntry.error}
		<div class="overlay error">Error: {layerEntry.error instanceof Error ? layerEntry.error.message : 'Unknown error'}</div>
	{:else if layerEntry.isLoadingGpu}
		<div class="overlay loading">Loading GPU resources...</div>
	{:else if !layerEntry.gpu}
		<div class="overlay loading">Waiting for GPU resources...</div>
	{/if}

	<!-- Canvas -->
	<canvas
		bind:this={canvasElement}
		class="view-canvas"
		onpointerdown={handlePointerDown}
		onpointermove={handlePointerMove}
		onpointerup={handlePointerUp}
		onpointerleave={handlePointerUp}
		onpointercancel={handlePointerUp}
		onwheel={handleWheel}
		data-testid={`canvas-${getViewTypeName(viewType).toLowerCase()}`}
	></canvas>

	<!-- Orientation indicators -->
	<div class="orientation-markers">
		{#if viewType === ViewType.Axial}
			<span class="marker top">A</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">P</span>
			<span class="marker left">R</span>
			<span class="marker right">L</span>
		{:else if viewType === ViewType.Coronal}
			<span class="marker top">S</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">I</span>
			<span class="marker left">R</span>
			<span class="marker right">L</span>
		{:else if viewType === ViewType.Sagittal}
			<span class="marker top">S</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">I</span>
			<span class="marker left">P</span>
			<span class="marker right">A</span>
		{/if}
	</div>
</div>

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/views/OrthogonalView.test.ts">
import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest';
import { render, cleanup } from '@testing-library/svelte';
import { crosshairSlice } from '$lib/stores/crosshairSlice';
import OrthogonalView from './OrthogonalView.svelte';
import { ViewType } from '$lib/types/ViewType';

// Mock ResizeObserver
global.ResizeObserver = vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
	observe: vi.fn(),
	disconnect: vi.fn(),
	unobserve: vi.fn()
}));

// Mock canvas getContext
HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({
	putImageData: vi.fn()
});

// Mock ImageData
global.ImageData = vi.fn().mockImplementation((data, width, height) => ({
	data,
	width,
	height
}));

// Mock the API
vi.mock('$lib/api', () => ({
	coreApi: {
		create_offscreen_render_target: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
		update_frame_ubo: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
		set_view_plane: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
		add_render_layer: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
		render_to_image: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue('data:image/raw-rgba;base64,'),
	}
}));

// Mock the layer store
vi.mock('$lib/stores/layerStore', () => ({
	useLayerStore: {
		getState: () => ({
			layers: [
				{
					spec: {
						Volume: {
							id: 'test-layer-1'
						}
					},
					gpu: {
						atlas_layer_index: 0,
						texture_coords: {
							u_min: 0,
							v_min: 0,
							u_max: 1,
							v_max: 1
						}
					}
				}
			]
		})
	}
}));

describe('OrthogonalView - Dynamic Slice Updates', () => {
	let updateFrameUboSpy: any;
	
	beforeEach(async () => {
		// Reset crosshair to default state
		crosshairSlice.setState({
			crosshairWorldCoord: [0, 0, 0],
			activeViewPlane: 0
		});
		
		// Get spy for update_frame_ubo
		const { coreApi } = await import('$lib/api');
		updateFrameUboSpy = vi.mocked(coreApi).update_frame_ubo;
		updateFrameUboSpy.mockClear();
	});
	
	afterEach(() => {
		cleanup();
		vi.clearAllMocks();
	});
	
	it('should update rendering when crosshair position changes', async () => {
		// Render the component
		const { container } = render(OrthogonalView, {
			props: {
				layerId: 'test-layer-1',
				viewType: ViewType.Axial
			}
		});
		
		// Wait for initial render
		await vi.waitFor(() => {
			expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		});
		
		// Clear the spy to track only new calls
		updateFrameUboSpy.mockClear();
		
		// Change crosshair position
		crosshairSlice.setState({
			crosshairWorldCoord: [10, 20, 30],
			activeViewPlane: 0
		});
		
		// Wait for the effect to trigger
		await vi.waitFor(() => {
			expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		});
		
		// Verify the update was called with new crosshair position
		const lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
		const crosshairVoxel = lastCall[2]; // Third argument is crosshairVoxel
		
		expect(crosshairVoxel).toEqual([10, 20, 30, 1]);
	});
	
	it('should update correct slice based on view type', async () => {
		// Set crosshair position before rendering
		crosshairSlice.setState({
			crosshairWorldCoord: [50, 60, 70],
			activeViewPlane: 0
		});
		
		// Test Axial view
		const { rerender } = render(OrthogonalView, {
			props: {
				layerId: 'test-layer-1',
				viewType: ViewType.Axial
			}
		});
		
		// Wait for initial render
		await vi.waitFor(() => {
			expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		});
		
		// Check view plane distance for Axial (Z coordinate)
		let lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
		let viewPlaneDistance = lastCall[4]; // Fifth argument
		expect(viewPlaneDistance).toBe(70); // Z coordinate for Axial
		
		// Test Coronal view
		updateFrameUboSpy.mockClear();
		await rerender({
			layerId: 'test-layer-1',
			viewType: ViewType.Coronal
		});
		
		// Wait a bit for render
		await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
		
		// The rerender should trigger setupViewRenderParams again
		expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
		viewPlaneDistance = lastCall[4];
		expect(viewPlaneDistance).toBe(60); // Y coordinate for Coronal
		
		// Test Sagittal view
		updateFrameUboSpy.mockClear();
		await rerender({
			layerId: 'test-layer-1',
			viewType: ViewType.Sagittal
		});
		
		// Wait a bit for render
		await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
		
		expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
		viewPlaneDistance = lastCall[4];
		expect(viewPlaneDistance).toBe(50); // X coordinate for Sagittal
	});
	
	it('should not update when crosshair is null', async () => {
		// Set crosshair to null
		crosshairSlice.setState({
			crosshairWorldCoord: null,
			activeViewPlane: 0
		});
		
		// Render the component
		render(OrthogonalView, {
			props: {
				layerId: 'test-layer-1',
				viewType: ViewType.Axial
			}
		});
		
		// Wait a bit
		await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
		
		// Initial setup might be called, but check that it uses default [0,0,0]
		if (updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length > 0) {
			const lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
			const crosshairVoxel = lastCall[2];
			expect(crosshairVoxel).toEqual([0, 0, 0, 1]); // Default position
		}
	});
	
	it('should handle rapid crosshair changes', async () => {
		// Render the component
		render(OrthogonalView, {
			props: {
				layerId: 'test-layer-1',
				viewType: ViewType.Axial
			}
		});
		
		// Wait for initial render
		await vi.waitFor(() => {
			expect(updateFrameUboSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
		});
		
		const initialCallCount = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length;
		
		// Rapidly change crosshair position
		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
			crosshairSlice.setState({
				crosshairWorldCoord: [i * 10, i * 20, i * 30],
				activeViewPlane: 0
			});
			// Small delay between changes
			await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 10));
		}
		
		// Wait for effects to settle
		await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
		
		// Should have received multiple updates
		expect(updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length).toBeGreaterThan(initialCallCount);
		
		// Check the last update has the final position
		const lastCall = updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls[updateFrameUboSpy.mock.calls.length - 1];
		const crosshairVoxel = lastCall[2];
		expect(crosshairVoxel).toEqual([40, 80, 120, 1]); // Final position
	});
});

describe('OrthogonalView - Crosshair Click Updates', () => {
	it('should update crosshair when clicking on the canvas', async () => {
		// TODO: Implement click-to-set-crosshair functionality
		// This test is a placeholder for future implementation
		expect(true).toBe(true);
	});
});

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/views/OrthogonalViewContainer.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
	/**
	 * @component OrthogonalViewContainer
	 * @description Container for managing three orthogonal views with shared GPU context
	 */
	import { onMount, onDestroy } from 'svelte';
	import { coreApi } from '$lib/api';
	import { useLayerStore } from '$lib/stores/layerStore';
	import { crosshairSlice } from '$lib/stores/crosshairSlice';
	import { ViewType } from '$lib/types/ViewType';
	import OrthogonalViewGPU from './OrthogonalViewGPU.svelte';

	// Props
	let {
		selectedLayerId
	}: {
		selectedLayerId: string | null;
	} = $props();

	// State
	let gpuInitialized = $state(false);
	let initError = $state<string | null>(null);
	let viewports = $state({
		[ViewType.Axial]: { scale: 1.0, offset: [0, 0] as [number, number] },
		[ViewType.Coronal]: { scale: 1.0, offset: [0, 0] as [number, number] },
		[ViewType.Sagittal]: { scale: 1.0, offset: [0, 0] as [number, number] }
	});

	// Initialize GPU context once for all views
	async function initializeGPUContext() {
		try {
			console.log('OrthogonalViewContainer: Checking GPU context...');
			// init_render_loop is idempotent, but let's not call it multiple times
			gpuInitialized = true;
			console.log('OrthogonalViewContainer: GPU context ready');
		} catch (err) {
			console.error('Failed to initialize GPU context:', err);
			initError = `Failed to initialize GPU: ${err}`;
		}
	}

	// Handle viewport changes from individual views
	function handleViewportChange(viewType: ViewType, viewport: { scale: number; offset: [number, number] }) {
		viewports[viewType] = viewport;
	}

	// Handle crosshair click in a view
	function handleCrosshairClick(viewType: ViewType, position: { x: number; y: number }) {
		// Convert 2D click position to 3D voxel coordinates based on view type
		const currentCrosshair = crosshairSlice.getState();
		
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// Click on axial view updates X and Y
				crosshairSlice.setState({
					x: Math.round(position.x),
					y: Math.round(position.y),
					z: currentCrosshair.z
				});
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// Click on coronal view updates X and Z
				crosshairSlice.setState({
					x: Math.round(position.x),
					y: currentCrosshair.y,
					z: Math.round(position.y)
				});
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// Click on sagittal view updates Y and Z
				crosshairSlice.setState({
					x: currentCrosshair.x,
					y: Math.round(position.x),
					z: Math.round(position.y)
				});
				break;
		}
	}

	// Synchronize view scales (optional feature)
	let syncViewScales = $state(true);
	
	$effect(() => {
		if (syncViewScales) {
			// When one view scale changes, update all views to match
			const maxScale = Math.max(
				viewports[ViewType.Axial].scale,
				viewports[ViewType.Coronal].scale,
				viewports[ViewType.Sagittal].scale
			);
			
			if (viewports[ViewType.Axial].scale !== maxScale ||
				viewports[ViewType.Coronal].scale !== maxScale ||
				viewports[ViewType.Sagittal].scale !== maxScale) {
				
				viewports = {
					[ViewType.Axial]: { ...viewports[ViewType.Axial], scale: maxScale },
					[ViewType.Coronal]: { ...viewports[ViewType.Coronal], scale: maxScale },
					[ViewType.Sagittal]: { ...viewports[ViewType.Sagittal], scale: maxScale }
				};
			}
		}
	});

	onMount(() => {
		console.log('OrthogonalViewContainer mounted');
		initializeGPUContext();
	});

	onDestroy(() => {
		// Cleanup is handled by individual views
	});
</script>

<div class="orthogonal-view-container">
	{#if initError}
		<div class="init-error">
			<h3>GPU Initialization Error</h3>
			<p>{initError}</p>
		</div>
	{:else if !gpuInitialized}
		<div class="init-loading">
			<p>Initializing GPU context...</p>
		</div>
	{:else}
		<div class="view-grid">
			<div class="view-panel axial">
				<OrthogonalViewGPU
					layerId={selectedLayerId}
					viewType={ViewType.Axial}
					onViewportChange={handleViewportChange}
				/>
			</div>
			
			<div class="view-panel coronal">
				<OrthogonalViewGPU
					layerId={selectedLayerId}
					viewType={ViewType.Coronal}
					onViewportChange={handleViewportChange}
				/>
			</div>
			
			<div class="view-panel sagittal">
				<OrthogonalViewGPU
					layerId={selectedLayerId}
					viewType={ViewType.Sagittal}
					onViewportChange={handleViewportChange}
				/>
			</div>
		</div>
		
		<div class="view-controls">
			<label>
				<input type="checkbox" bind:checked={syncViewScales} />
				Synchronize view scales
			</label>
		</div>
	{/if}
</div>

		right: 1rem;
		background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
		padding: 0.5rem 1rem;
		border-radius: 4px;
		color: #fff;
		font-size: 14px;
		z-index: 100;
	}

	.view-controls label {
		display: flex;
		align-items: center;
		gap: 0.5rem;
		cursor: pointer;
		user-select: none;
	}

	.view-controls input[type="checkbox"] {
		cursor: pointer;
	}

	/* Responsive layout for smaller screens */
	@media (max-width: 768px) {
		.view-grid {
			grid-template-columns: 1fr;
			grid-template-rows: repeat(3, 1fr);
		}

		.view-panel.axial {
			grid-column: 1;
			grid-row: 1;
		}

		.view-panel.coronal {
			grid-column: 1;
			grid-row: 2;
		}

		.view-panel.sagittal {
			grid-column: 1;
			grid-row: 3;
		}
	}
</style>

<file path="ui/src/lib/components/views/OrthogonalViewGPU.svelte">
<script lang="ts">
	/**
	 * @component OrthogonalViewGPU
	 * @description GPU-accelerated orthogonal view canvas connected to the WebGPU render loop
	 */
	import { onMount, onDestroy } from 'svelte';
	import { debounce } from 'lodash-es';
	import { coreApi } from '$lib/api';
	import { useLayerStore, type LayerEntry } from '$lib/stores/layerStore';
	import { crosshairSlice } from '$lib/stores/crosshairSlice';
	import { ViewType, getViewTypeName } from '$lib/types/ViewType';
	import type { VolumeLayerGpuInfo, LayerSpec } from '$lib/api';

	// Props
	let { 
		layerId,
		viewType,
		onViewportChange
	}: { 
		layerId: string | null;
		viewType: ViewType;
		onViewportChange?: (viewType: ViewType, viewport: { scale: number; offset: [number, number] }) => void;
	} = $props();

	// State
	let canvasElement = $state<HTMLCanvasElement>();
	let viewScale = $state(1.0);
	let viewOffset = $state([0, 0] as [number, number]);
	let isDragging = $state(false);
	let lastPointerPos = $state<[number, number] | null>(null);
	let containerSize = $state({ width: 512, height: 512 });
	let isRenderLoopRunning = $state(false);
	let animationFrameId: number | null = null;
	let gpuInitialized = $state(false);
	let renderTargetCreated = $state(false);
	
	// Track view center separately from crosshair
	let viewCenter = $state<[number, number, number] | null>(null);
	
	// Slice position state
	let slicePosition = $state(0); // Current slice position in mm
	let sliceMin = $state(-100); // Min slice position
	let sliceMax = $state(100); // Max slice position
	let sliceStep = $state(1); // Step size in mm

	// Helper to get layer ID from spec
	function getLayerIdFromSpec(spec: LayerSpec): string | null {
		if (spec && 'Volume' in spec) {
			return spec.Volume.id;
		}
		return null;
	}
	
	// Store state - make it reactive
	let layerStoreState = $state(useLayerStore.getState());
	
	// Get layer from store using a derived value for robust reactivity
	let layerEntry = $derived(
		layerId ? layerStoreState.layers.find((l: LayerEntry) => getLayerIdFromSpec(l.spec) === layerId) || null : null
	);
	

	// Get current crosshair position - make it reactive
	let crosshairState = $state(crosshairSlice.getState());
	// Default to null if no crosshair set (will be updated when volume loads)
	let crosshairPos = $derived(crosshairState.crosshairWorldCoord);

	// Canvas resize observer
	let resizeObserver: ResizeObserver | null = null;

	// Initialize GPU rendering
	async function initializeGPU() {
		try {
			if (!gpuInitialized) {
				console.log('Initializing GPU render loop...');
				await coreApi.init_render_loop();
				gpuInitialized = true;
			}
			
			// Create render target for this view
			// DISABLED: The main layout already creates a 512x512 render target
			// TODO: Support multiple render targets or dynamic sizing
			if (!renderTargetCreated && containerSize.width > 0 && containerSize.height > 0) {
				// The main layout creates a 512x512 render target
				// TODO: Support multiple render targets or dynamic sizing
				renderTargetCreated = true;
			}
		} catch (err) {
			console.error('Failed to initialize GPU:', err);
		}
	}

	// Setup view-specific parameters
	async function setupViewParameters() {
		if (!layerEntry?.gpu) return;
		
		// Wait for crosshair to be initialized
		if (!crosshairPos) {
			console.log('[OrthogonalViewGPU] Crosshair not yet initialized, skipping setup');
			return;
		}
		
		// Initialize view center if not set
		if (!viewCenter) {
			viewCenter = [...crosshairPos];
			console.log('[OrthogonalViewGPU] Initialized view center to:', viewCenter);
		}

		const { width, height } = containerSize;
		const aspectRatio = width / height;
		
		// Create orthographic projection matrix based on view scale and offset
		const scale = viewScale * 2;
		const viewProj = [
			scale / width, 0, 0, 0,
			0, scale / height, 0, 0,
			0, 0, 1, 0,
			-viewOffset[0] * scale / width, -viewOffset[1] * scale / height, 0, 1
		];

		// Get volume dimensions from layer info
		const volumeDim = layerEntry.gpu.dim;
		
		// DEBUG: Log the actual GPU metadata
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Volume metadata from GPU:`, {
			dim: [...layerEntry.gpu.dim],  // Spread to unwrap proxy
			spacing: [...layerEntry.gpu.spacing],  // Spread to unwrap proxy
			origin: [...layerEntry.gpu.origin]  // Spread to unwrap proxy
		});
		
		// Calculate view plane normal and distance based on view type
		let viewPlaneNormal: number[];
		let viewPlaneDistance: number;
		let viewPlaneIndex: number;
		
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				viewPlaneNormal = [0, 0, 1, 0];
				viewPlaneDistance = crosshairPos[2];
				viewPlaneIndex = 2;
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				viewPlaneNormal = [0, 1, 0, 0];
				viewPlaneDistance = crosshairPos[1];
				viewPlaneIndex = 1;
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				viewPlaneNormal = [1, 0, 0, 0];
				viewPlaneDistance = crosshairPos[0];
				viewPlaneIndex = 0;
				break;
		}

		// Update frame for synchronized view
		// Map ViewType to plane_id (0=Axial, 1=Coronal, 2=Sagittal)
		const planeId = viewType as 0 | 1 | 2;
		
		// Calculate view dimensions in mm based on actual volume dimensions
		// Get volume bounds from GPU info
		const volumeDims = layerEntry.gpu.dim;
		const volumeSpacing = layerEntry.gpu.spacing;
		const volumeOrigin = layerEntry.gpu.origin;
		
		// Calculate volume bounds in world space (mm)
		// The spacing can be positive or negative to indicate direction
		const volumeBounds = {
			x: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[0], volumeOrigin[0] + (volumeDims[0] - 1) * volumeSpacing[0]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[0], volumeOrigin[0] + (volumeDims[0] - 1) * volumeSpacing[0]) },
			y: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[1], volumeOrigin[1] + (volumeDims[1] - 1) * volumeSpacing[1]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[1], volumeOrigin[1] + (volumeDims[1] - 1) * volumeSpacing[1]) },
			z: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[2], volumeOrigin[2] + (volumeDims[2] - 1) * volumeSpacing[2]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[2], volumeOrigin[2] + (volumeDims[2] - 1) * volumeSpacing[2]) }
		};
		
		// DEBUG: Log the calculated bounds
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Calculated volume bounds:`, {
			x: `[${volumeBounds.x.min.toFixed(3)}, ${volumeBounds.x.max.toFixed(3)}]`,
			y: `[${volumeBounds.y.min.toFixed(3)}, ${volumeBounds.y.max.toFixed(3)}]`,
			z: `[${volumeBounds.z.min.toFixed(3)}, ${volumeBounds.z.max.toFixed(3)}]`
		});
		
		// Calculate the natural dimensions for each view type
		let naturalWidthMm: number;
		let naturalHeightMm: number;
		
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// XY plane
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.x.max - volumeBounds.x.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.y.max - volumeBounds.y.min;
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// XZ plane
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.x.max - volumeBounds.x.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.z.max - volumeBounds.z.min;
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// YZ plane
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.y.max - volumeBounds.y.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.z.max - volumeBounds.z.min;
				break;
		}
		
		// DEBUG: Log natural dimensions before padding
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Natural dimensions before padding: ${naturalWidthMm.toFixed(3)} x ${naturalHeightMm.toFixed(3)} mm`);
		
		// Add some padding (10% on each side)
		const padding = 1.2;
		naturalWidthMm *= padding;
		naturalHeightMm *= padding;
		
		// DEBUG: Log after padding
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] After padding (${padding}x): ${naturalWidthMm.toFixed(3)} x ${naturalHeightMm.toFixed(3)} mm`);
		
		// Apply view scale (zoom)
		const viewWidthMm = naturalWidthMm / viewScale;
		const viewHeightMm = naturalHeightMm / viewScale;
		
		// DEBUG: Log final view dimensions
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Final view dimensions (scale=${viewScale}): ${viewWidthMm.toFixed(3)} x ${viewHeightMm.toFixed(3)} mm`);
		
		// Update slice bounds based on view type
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// Z axis (Inferior to Superior)
				sliceMin = volumeBounds.z.min;
				sliceMax = volumeBounds.z.max;
				slicePosition = crosshairPos[2];
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// Y axis (Posterior to Anterior)
				sliceMin = volumeBounds.y.min;
				sliceMax = volumeBounds.y.max;
				slicePosition = crosshairPos[1];
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// X axis (Right to Left)
				sliceMin = volumeBounds.x.min;
				sliceMax = volumeBounds.x.max;
				slicePosition = crosshairPos[0];
				break;
		}
		
		// Log current crosshair position for debugging
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Setting up view for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}`);
		console.log(`  Crosshair position: [${crosshairPos[0]}, ${crosshairPos[1]}, ${crosshairPos[2]}]`);
		console.log(`  View dimensions: ${viewWidthMm.toFixed(1)} x ${viewHeightMm.toFixed(1)} mm`);
		console.log(`  Volume bounds:`, volumeBounds);
		
		// TEMPORARY FIX: Force crosshair to volume center if it's outside bounds
		const volumeCenterX = (volumeBounds.x.min + volumeBounds.x.max) / 2;
		const volumeCenterY = (volumeBounds.y.min + volumeBounds.y.max) / 2;
		const volumeCenterZ = (volumeBounds.z.min + volumeBounds.z.max) / 2;
		
		if (crosshairPos[0] < volumeBounds.x.min || crosshairPos[0] > volumeBounds.x.max ||
		    crosshairPos[1] < volumeBounds.y.min || crosshairPos[1] > volumeBounds.y.max ||
		    crosshairPos[2] < volumeBounds.z.min || crosshairPos[2] > volumeBounds.z.max) {
			console.warn(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Crosshair outside volume bounds! Resetting to center: [${volumeCenterX}, ${volumeCenterY}, ${volumeCenterZ}]`);
			crosshairSlice.getState().setCrosshairWorldCoord([volumeCenterX, volumeCenterY, volumeCenterZ]);
			return; // Skip this frame, will re-render with correct crosshair
		}
		
		// Set view plane first
		await coreApi.set_view_plane(planeId);
		
		// Set crosshair position for the shader
		// Use the actual slice position, not the original crosshair position
		let shaderCrosshair: [number, number, number];
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// Update Z to slice position
				shaderCrosshair = [crosshairPos[0], crosshairPos[1], slicePosition];
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// Update Y to slice position
				shaderCrosshair = [crosshairPos[0], slicePosition, crosshairPos[2]];
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// Update X to slice position
				shaderCrosshair = [slicePosition, crosshairPos[1], crosshairPos[2]];
				break;
		}
		await coreApi.set_crosshair(shaderCrosshair);
		
		
		// For now, pass the natural dimensions without adjustment
		// TODO: The aspect ratio preservation should be handled in the renderer
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] View dimensions:`, {
			width: viewWidthMm.toFixed(3),
			height: viewHeightMm.toFixed(3),
			aspect: (viewWidthMm / viewHeightMm).toFixed(3),
			viewport: { width: containerSize.width, height: containerSize.height, aspect: (containerSize.width / containerSize.height).toFixed(3) }
		});
		
		// Use view center for in-plane coordinates, slicePosition for slice coordinate
		let frameCenter: [number, number, number];
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// Keep X,Y from view center, use slicePosition for Z
				frameCenter = [viewCenter[0], viewCenter[1], slicePosition];
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// Keep X,Z from view center, use slicePosition for Y
				frameCenter = [viewCenter[0], slicePosition, viewCenter[2]];
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// Keep Y,Z from view center, use slicePosition for X
				frameCenter = [slicePosition, viewCenter[1], viewCenter[2]];
				break;
		}
		
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Frame center for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}:`, frameCenter);
		console.log(`  View center: [${viewCenter[0].toFixed(1)}, ${viewCenter[1].toFixed(1)}, ${viewCenter[2].toFixed(1)}]`);
		console.log(`  Slice position: ${slicePosition.toFixed(1)}`);
		console.log(`  Crosshair shader: [${shaderCrosshair[0].toFixed(1)}, ${shaderCrosshair[1].toFixed(1)}, ${shaderCrosshair[2].toFixed(1)}]`);
		
		await coreApi.update_frame_for_synchronized_view(
			viewWidthMm,
			viewHeightMm,
			frameCenter,
			planeId
		);
	}

	// Track if layer is already added
	let layerAdded = false;
	
	// Add layer to render state
	async function addLayerToRenderState() {
		if (!layerEntry?.gpu || !renderTargetCreated || layerAdded) return;
		

		try {
			// Add render layer with correct parameters
			const textureCoords = [
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.u_min,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.v_min,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.u_max,
				layerEntry.gpu.texture_coords.v_max
			];
			
			await coreApi.add_render_layer(
				layerEntry.gpu.atlas_layer_index,
				1.0, // opacity
				textureCoords
			);
			
			layerAdded = true;
		} catch (err) {
			console.error('Failed to add layer to render state:', err);
			// If we get a max layer error, it might mean the layer was already added
			if (err && typeof err === 'object' && 'Internal' in err) {
				const internal = err.Internal;
				if (internal && internal.details && internal.details.includes('Maximum layer count exceeded')) {
					console.warn('Layer might already be added, continuing...');
					layerAdded = true;
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// Render frame
	async function renderFrame() {
		if (!canvasElement || !layerEntry?.gpu || !renderTargetCreated) return;
		
		// Ensure we have valid dimensions
		if (containerSize.width === 0 || containerSize.height === 0) {
			console.warn('Skipping render - container size is 0x0');
			return;
		}
		
		// Wait for crosshair to be initialized
		if (!crosshairPos) {
			console.log('[OrthogonalViewGPU] Skipping render - crosshair not initialized');
			return;
		}
		

		try {
			// Update view parameters
			await setupViewParameters();
			
			// Render to offscreen buffer and get image
			const imageDataUrl = await coreApi.render_to_image();
			
			// Parse the data URL
			if (imageDataUrl.startsWith('data:image/raw-rgba;base64,')) {
				const base64Data = imageDataUrl.substring('data:image/raw-rgba;base64,'.length);
				const binaryData = atob(base64Data);
				const bytes = new Uint8Array(binaryData.length);
				for (let i = 0; i < binaryData.length; i++) {
					bytes[i] = binaryData.charCodeAt(i);
				}

				// Get canvas context
				const ctx = canvasElement.getContext('2d');
				if (!ctx) return;

				// Validate buffer size
				// Note: The render loop might return a fixed size buffer (e.g., 352x256)
				// regardless of what we request
				const expectedSize = containerSize.width * containerSize.height * 4; // RGBA = 4 bytes per pixel
				const actualPixelCount = bytes.length / 4;
				
				if (bytes.length !== expectedSize) {
					console.warn(`Buffer size mismatch: got ${bytes.length} bytes, canvas: ${containerSize.width}x${containerSize.height} - will scale to fit`);
					
					// Calculate actual render dimensions from buffer size
					// Buffer size = width * height * 4 (RGBA)
					const pixelCount = bytes.length / 4;
					
					// Try common aspect ratios and sizes
					let renderWidth = 0;
					let renderHeight = 0;
					
					// Try some common dimensions
					const possibleSizes = [
						[512, 512], [256, 256], [352, 256], [256, 352],
						[512, 256], [256, 512], [512, 176], [176, 512],
						[352, 256] // Exact match for 360448 bytes
					];
					
					for (const [w, h] of possibleSizes) {
						if (w * h === pixelCount) {
							renderWidth = w;
							renderHeight = h;
							break;
						}
					}
					
					// If not found, try to find any valid dimensions
					if (renderWidth === 0) {
						// Try dividing by common widths
						for (const width of [512, 256, 352, 480, 640]) {
							if (pixelCount % width === 0) {
								const height = pixelCount / width;
								if (height === Math.floor(height)) {
									renderWidth = width;
									renderHeight = height;
									break;
								}
							}
						}
					}
					
					if (renderWidth > 0 && renderHeight > 0) {
						// Create ImageData with render target dimensions
						const imageData = new ImageData(
							new Uint8ClampedArray(bytes.buffer),
							renderWidth,
							renderHeight
						);
						
						// Check for content in the rendered image
						const pixels = new Uint8ClampedArray(bytes.buffer);
						let hasContent = false;
						let uniqueColors = new Set();
						let samplePixels = [];
						
						// Sample some pixels to check for variation
						for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(pixels.length, 10000); i += 4) {
							const color = `${pixels[i]},${pixels[i+1]},${pixels[i+2]}`;
							uniqueColors.add(color);
							if (samplePixels.length < 5) {
								samplePixels.push(`[${i/4}]: R=${pixels[i]} G=${pixels[i+1]} B=${pixels[i+2]} A=${pixels[i+3]}`);
							}
							if (pixels[i] > 0 || pixels[i+1] > 0 || pixels[i+2] > 0) {
								hasContent = true;
							}
						}
						
						// Scale to fit canvas
						const tempCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
						tempCanvas.width = renderWidth;
						tempCanvas.height = renderHeight;
						const tempCtx = tempCanvas.getContext('2d');
						if (tempCtx) {
							tempCtx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
							// Clear canvas first
							ctx.clearRect(0, 0, containerSize.width, containerSize.height);
							
							// Calculate aspect-preserving dimensions
							const sourceAspect = renderWidth / renderHeight;
							const targetAspect = containerSize.width / containerSize.height;
							
							let drawWidth = containerSize.width;
							let drawHeight = containerSize.height;
							let drawX = 0;
							let drawY = 0;
							
							if (sourceAspect > targetAspect) {
								// Source is wider - fit width, letterbox height
								drawHeight = containerSize.width / sourceAspect;
								drawY = (containerSize.height - drawHeight) / 2;
							} else if (sourceAspect < targetAspect) {
								// Source is taller - fit height, pillarbox width
								drawWidth = containerSize.height * sourceAspect;
								drawX = (containerSize.width - drawWidth) / 2;
							}
							
							// Draw scaled image with aspect ratio preserved
							ctx.drawImage(tempCanvas, 0, 0, renderWidth, renderHeight, drawX, drawY, drawWidth, drawHeight);
						}
					} else {
						console.warn('Could not determine buffer dimensions for size:', bytes.length, '- skipping render');
					}
				} else {
					// Create ImageData from raw RGBA
					const imageData = new ImageData(
						new Uint8ClampedArray(bytes.buffer),
						containerSize.width,
						containerSize.height
					);
					
					// Debug: Check if image has any non-zero pixels
					const pixels = new Uint8ClampedArray(bytes.buffer);
					let hasContent = false;
					for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(100, pixels.length); i += 4) {
						if (pixels[i] > 0 || pixels[i+1] > 0 || pixels[i+2] > 0) {
							hasContent = true;
							break;
						}
					}
					

					// Draw to canvas with aspect ratio preservation
					// Since render target is 512x512 (square), preserve aspect ratio
					const sourceAspect = 1.0; // 512x512 is square
					const targetAspect = containerSize.width / containerSize.height;
					
					let drawWidth = containerSize.width;
					let drawHeight = containerSize.height;
					let drawX = 0;
					let drawY = 0;
					
					if (sourceAspect > targetAspect) {
						// Source is wider - fit width, letterbox height
						drawHeight = containerSize.width / sourceAspect;
						drawY = (containerSize.height - drawHeight) / 2;
					} else if (sourceAspect < targetAspect) {
						// Source is taller - fit height, pillarbox width
						drawWidth = containerSize.height * sourceAspect;
						drawX = (containerSize.width - drawWidth) / 2;
					}
					
					// Create temp canvas to hold the image data
					const tempCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
					tempCanvas.width = containerSize.width;
					tempCanvas.height = containerSize.height;
					const tempCtx = tempCanvas.getContext('2d');
					if (tempCtx) {
						tempCtx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
						
						// Clear and draw with proper aspect ratio
						ctx.clearRect(0, 0, containerSize.width, containerSize.height);
						ctx.drawImage(tempCanvas, 0, 0, containerSize.width, containerSize.height, drawX, drawY, drawWidth, drawHeight);
					}
				}
			}
		} catch (err) {
			console.error('Render frame error:', err);
		}
	}

	// Start render loop
	function startRenderLoop() {
		if (isRenderLoopRunning) return;
		
		isRenderLoopRunning = true;
		
		// Render once immediately
		renderFrame().then(() => {
			// For now, don't use continuous animation - just render on changes
			// We'll add proper dirty tracking later
		});
	}

	// Stop render loop
	function stopRenderLoop() {
		isRenderLoopRunning = false;
		if (animationFrameId !== null) {
			cancelAnimationFrame(animationFrameId);
			animationFrameId = null;
		}
	}

	// Debounced resize handler
	const handleResizeDebounced = debounce(async (width: number, height: number) => {
		const newWidth = Math.max(256, Math.floor(width));
		const newHeight = Math.max(256, Math.floor(height));
		
		if (newWidth > 0 && newHeight > 0 && (newWidth !== containerSize.width || newHeight !== containerSize.height)) {
			containerSize = { width: newWidth, height: newHeight };
			
			// Update canvas size
			if (canvasElement) {
				canvasElement.width = newWidth;
				canvasElement.height = newHeight;
			}
			
			// Recreate render target with new size
			renderTargetCreated = false;
			await initializeGPU();
			
			// Re-render with new size
			if (isRenderLoopRunning && layerEntry?.gpu) {
				await renderFrame();
			}
		}
	}, 250);

	// Convert canvas coordinates to world coordinates
	function canvasToWorld(canvasX: number, canvasY: number): [number, number, number] | null {
		if (!layerEntry?.gpu || !crosshairPos) return null;
		
		// Get canvas dimensions
		const rect = canvasElement?.getBoundingClientRect();
		if (!rect) return null;
		
		// Convert to normalized device coordinates (-1 to 1)
		const ndcX = (canvasX / rect.width) * 2 - 1;
		const ndcY = -((canvasY / rect.height) * 2 - 1); // Flip Y
		
		// Get view parameters
		const volumeDims = layerEntry.gpu.dim;
		const volumeSpacing = layerEntry.gpu.spacing;
		const volumeOrigin = layerEntry.gpu.origin;
		
		// Calculate volume bounds
		const volumeBounds = {
			x: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[0], volumeOrigin[0] + (volumeDims[0] - 1) * volumeSpacing[0]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[0], volumeOrigin[0] + (volumeDims[0] - 1) * volumeSpacing[0]) },
			y: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[1], volumeOrigin[1] + (volumeDims[1] - 1) * volumeSpacing[1]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[1], volumeOrigin[1] + (volumeDims[1] - 1) * volumeSpacing[1]) },
			z: { min: Math.min(volumeOrigin[2], volumeOrigin[2] + (volumeDims[2] - 1) * volumeSpacing[2]),
			     max: Math.max(volumeOrigin[2], volumeOrigin[2] + (volumeDims[2] - 1) * volumeSpacing[2]) }
		};
		
		// Get natural dimensions for this view
		let naturalWidthMm: number;
		let naturalHeightMm: number;
		
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.x.max - volumeBounds.x.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.y.max - volumeBounds.y.min;
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.x.max - volumeBounds.x.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.z.max - volumeBounds.z.min;
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				naturalWidthMm = volumeBounds.y.max - volumeBounds.y.min;
				naturalHeightMm = volumeBounds.z.max - volumeBounds.z.min;
				break;
		}
		
		// Add padding
		const padding = 1.2;
		naturalWidthMm *= padding;
		naturalHeightMm *= padding;
		
		// Apply view scale
		const viewWidthMm = naturalWidthMm / viewScale;
		const viewHeightMm = naturalHeightMm / viewScale;
		
		// Convert NDC to world coordinates based on view type
		const halfWidth = viewWidthMm / 2;
		const halfHeight = viewHeightMm / 2;
		
		let worldX = crosshairPos[0];
		let worldY = crosshairPos[1];
		let worldZ = crosshairPos[2];
		
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				// XY plane - mouse moves X and Y
				worldX = crosshairPos[0] + ndcX * halfWidth;
				worldY = crosshairPos[1] - ndcY * halfHeight; // Negative because Y is flipped
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				// XZ plane - mouse moves X and Z
				worldX = crosshairPos[0] + ndcX * halfWidth;
				worldZ = crosshairPos[2] - ndcY * halfHeight; // Negative because Z is flipped
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				// YZ plane - mouse moves Y and Z
				worldY = crosshairPos[1] + ndcX * halfWidth;
				worldZ = crosshairPos[2] - ndcY * halfHeight; // Negative because Z is flipped
				break;
		}
		
		return [worldX, worldY, worldZ];
	}

	// Mouse/pointer handlers
	function handlePointerDown(event: PointerEvent) {
		if (event.button === 0) { // Left click
			// Calculate mouse position relative to canvas
			const rect = canvasElement?.getBoundingClientRect();
			if (rect) {
				const canvasX = event.clientX - rect.left;
				const canvasY = event.clientY - rect.top;
				
				// Convert to world coordinates and update crosshair
				const worldCoords = canvasToWorld(canvasX, canvasY);
				if (worldCoords) {
					crosshairSlice.getState().setCrosshairWorldCoord(worldCoords);
				}
			}
			
			// Start dragging for pan - DISABLED for now
			// isDragging = true;
			// lastPointerPos = [event.clientX, event.clientY];
			// (event.target as HTMLElement).setPointerCapture(event.pointerId);
		}
	}

	function handlePointerMove(event: PointerEvent) {
		// Update mouse world coordinates for status bar
		const rect = canvasElement?.getBoundingClientRect();
		if (rect) {
			const canvasX = event.clientX - rect.left;
			const canvasY = event.clientY - rect.top;
			const worldCoords = canvasToWorld(canvasX, canvasY);
			
			// Dispatch custom event for StatusBar
			if (worldCoords) {
				window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mouseworldcoord', { 
					detail: worldCoords 
				}));
			}
		}
		
		// Handle dragging for pan - DISABLED for now
		// if (isDragging && lastPointerPos) {
		// 	const dx = event.clientX - lastPointerPos[0];
		// 	const dy = event.clientY - lastPointerPos[1];
		// 	
		// 	viewOffset = [
		// 		viewOffset[0] + dx / viewScale,
		// 		viewOffset[1] + dy / viewScale
		// 	];
		// 	
		// 	lastPointerPos = [event.clientX, event.clientY];
		// 	
		// 	onViewportChange?.(viewType, { scale: viewScale, offset: viewOffset });
		// }
	}

	function handlePointerUp(event: PointerEvent) {
		isDragging = false;
		lastPointerPos = null;
		(event.target as HTMLElement).releasePointerCapture(event.pointerId);
	}
	
	function handlePointerLeave(event: PointerEvent) {
		// Clear mouse coordinates when leaving canvas
		window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('mouseworldcoord', { 
			detail: null 
		}));
	}

	function handleWheel(event: WheelEvent) {
		event.preventDefault();
		const scaleFactor = event.deltaY > 0 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
		viewScale = Math.max(0.1, Math.min(10, viewScale * scaleFactor));
		
		onViewportChange?.(viewType, { scale: viewScale, offset: viewOffset });
	}
	
	// Handle slider change
	function handleSliderChange(event: Event) {
		const target = event.target as HTMLInputElement;
		const newPosition = parseFloat(target.value);
		
		// Skip if crosshair not initialized
		if (!crosshairPos) return;
		
		// Debug logging
		console.log(`[OrthogonalViewGPU] Slider change for ${getViewTypeName(viewType)}`);
		console.log(`  Old crosshair: [${crosshairPos[0].toFixed(1)}, ${crosshairPos[1].toFixed(1)}, ${crosshairPos[2].toFixed(1)}]`);
		console.log(`  New slider position: ${newPosition.toFixed(1)}`);
		
		// Update crosshair position based on view type
		let newCrosshair: [number, number, number] = [...crosshairPos];
		switch (viewType) {
			case ViewType.Axial:
				newCrosshair[2] = newPosition;
				break;
			case ViewType.Coronal:
				newCrosshair[1] = newPosition;
				break;
			case ViewType.Sagittal:
				newCrosshair[0] = newPosition;
				break;
		}
		
		console.log(`  New crosshair: [${newCrosshair[0].toFixed(1)}, ${newCrosshair[1].toFixed(1)}, ${newCrosshair[2].toFixed(1)}]`);
		
		// For now, don't update global crosshair - just update local slice position
		// This prevents other views from jumping when we move the slider
		// crosshairSlice.getState().setCrosshairWorldCoord(newCrosshair);
		
		// Update local slice position immediately for responsive UI
		slicePosition = newPosition;
		
		// Trigger a re-render with the new slice position
		if (isRenderLoopRunning && layerEntry?.gpu) {
			setupViewParameters().then(() => {
				renderFrame();
			});
		}
	}

	// React to layer changes
	$effect(() => {
		if (layerEntry?.gpu && gpuInitialized && renderTargetCreated && containerSize.width > 0 && containerSize.height > 0) {
			// Reset layer added flag if layer changed
			const currentLayerId = getLayerIdFromSpec(layerEntry.spec);
			if (currentLayerId !== layerId) {
				layerAdded = false;
			}
			
			addLayerToRenderState().then(() => {
				startRenderLoop();
			});
		} else {
			stopRenderLoop();
		}
	});

	// React to crosshair changes
	$effect(() => {
		// Update view parameters and re-render when crosshair changes
		if (isRenderLoopRunning && layerEntry?.gpu && crosshairPos) {
			console.log('[OrthogonalViewGPU] Crosshair changed, updating view parameters');
			setupViewParameters().then(() => {
				renderFrame();
			});
		}
	});

	// Lifecycle
	onMount(async () => {
		// Subscribe to store changes
		const unsubscribeLayerStore = useLayerStore.subscribe((state) => {
			layerStoreState = state;
		});
		
		// Subscribe to crosshair changes
		const unsubscribeCrosshair = crosshairSlice.subscribe((state) => {
			crosshairState = state;
		});
		
		if (canvasElement) {
			// Wait a frame to ensure the layout is complete
			await new Promise(resolve => requestAnimationFrame(resolve));
			
			// Try getting dimensions from parent container first
			const parent = canvasElement.parentElement;
			const parentRect = parent?.getBoundingClientRect();
			
			// Set initial canvas size
			const rect = canvasElement.getBoundingClientRect();
			const width = Math.max(256, Math.floor(parentRect?.width || rect.width || 256));
			const height = Math.max(256, Math.floor(parentRect?.height || rect.height || 256));
			
			canvasElement.width = width;
			canvasElement.height = height;
			containerSize = { width, height };
			
			// Initialize GPU after we have dimensions
			await initializeGPU();
			
			// Setup resize observer
			resizeObserver = new ResizeObserver((entries) => {
				const entry = entries[0];
				const { width, height } = entry.contentRect;
				handleResizeDebounced(Math.floor(width), Math.floor(height));
			});
			resizeObserver.observe(canvasElement.parentElement || canvasElement);
		}
		
		return () => {
			stopRenderLoop();
			resizeObserver?.disconnect();
			handleResizeDebounced.cancel();
			unsubscribeLayerStore();
			unsubscribeCrosshair();
			
			// Clean up GPU resources
			if (layerId && layerEntry?.gpu) {
				// TODO: Add API call to remove render layer when available
				// coreApi.remove_render_layer(layerId).catch(console.error);
			}
		};
	});
</script>

<div class="orthogonal-view" data-view-type={viewType}>
	<!-- View label -->
	<div class="view-label">{getViewTypeName(viewType)}</div>
	
	<!-- Error/loading overlays -->
	{#if !layerEntry}
		<div class="overlay loading">No layer selected</div>
	{:else if layerEntry.error}
		<div class="overlay error">Error: {layerEntry.error instanceof Error ? layerEntry.error.message : 'Unknown error'}</div>
	{:else if layerEntry.isLoadingGpu}
		<div class="overlay loading">Loading GPU resources...</div>
	{:else if !layerEntry.gpu}
		<div class="overlay loading">Waiting for GPU resources...</div>
	{:else if !gpuInitialized}
		<div class="overlay loading">Initializing GPU...</div>
	{:else}
		{#if !renderTargetCreated}
			<div class="overlay loading">Creating render target...</div>
		{/if}
	{/if}

	<!-- Canvas -->
	<canvas
		bind:this={canvasElement}
		class="view-canvas"
		onpointerdown={handlePointerDown}
		onpointermove={handlePointerMove}
		onpointerup={handlePointerUp}
		onpointerleave={handlePointerLeave}
		onpointercancel={handlePointerUp}
		onwheel={handleWheel}
		data-testid={`canvas-${getViewTypeName(viewType).toLowerCase()}`}
	></canvas>

	<!-- Orientation indicators -->
	<div class="orientation-markers">
		{#if viewType === ViewType.Axial}
			<span class="marker top">A</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">P</span>
			<span class="marker left">R</span>
			<span class="marker right">L</span>
		{:else if viewType === ViewType.Coronal}
			<span class="marker top">S</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">I</span>
			<span class="marker left">R</span>
			<span class="marker right">L</span>
		{:else if viewType === ViewType.Sagittal}
			<span class="marker top">S</span>
			<span class="marker bottom">I</span>
			<span class="marker left">P</span>
			<span class="marker right">A</span>
		{/if}
	</div>
	
	<!-- Slice slider -->
	{#if layerEntry?.gpu}
		<div class="slice-slider-container">
			<div class="slice-info">
				<span class="slice-label">
					{#if viewType === ViewType.Axial}
						I/S:
					{:else if viewType === ViewType.Coronal}
						P/A:
					{:else}
						R/L:
					{/if}
				</span>
				<span class="slice-value">{slicePosition.toFixed(1)} mm</span>
			</div>
			<input
				type="range"
				class="slice-slider"
				min={sliceMin}
				max={sliceMax}
				step={sliceStep}
				value={slicePosition}
				oninput={handleSliderChange}
			/>
		</div>
	{/if}
</div>
